cover_letter:
Dear Vishakha and Hiring Team,

When Foxtel needed someone to build the business case, design the pilot framework, and deliver a working Gen AI solution integrating customer segmentation with LLM-assisted analysis — that fell to me. That hands-on accountability across strategy, architecture, and delivery is precisely what the AI Lead role demands, and it is where I operate best.

Across 20 years leading data and analytics delivery in financial services, government, and enterprise media, I have consistently sat at the intersection of ambiguous business problems and structured, executable solutions. I translate stakeholder complexity into actionable designs — and I back that with genuine technical depth. I have built and deployed working applications using Claude AI, Claude Code, Claude Design, Streamlit, and GitHub, and I actively use King AI in my workflows. I am ready and willing to demonstrate these applications directly to your team.

Three capabilities I would bring immediately to this engagement:

**AI delivery ownership** — At Foxtel, I owned the end-to-end Gen AI initiative: business case, stakeholder governance, pilot design, and evaluation framework. I did not advise from the side — I led it.

**Executive-ready consulting rigour** — At MIP, I managed 10+ concurrent client engagements for C-suite stakeholders including Sydney Water and NSW Health, producing structured solution documentation, requirements artefacts, and delivery governance across regulated environments. That consulting discipline translates directly to Big 4-calibre engagement quality.

**Technical credibility with AI tooling** — Beyond strategy, I write Python automation, build Streamlit dashboards, engineer prompts, and work hands-on with LLM interfaces. I interrogate solutions from first principles — not just present them.

I understand this role requires depth in RAG architectures and agentic frameworks that I am actively building; I am pursuing targeted AI architecture certifications and bring the data engineering foundations — Snowflake, SQL, cloud pipelines — that make that acceleration credible and fast.

I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my background fits your client's needs.

Kind regards,

Madeli van Niekerk
0424 998 470

cover_letter:
Dear Hiring Manager,

Turning complex data into clear, forward-looking insights that help risk teams make better decisions — that is precisely where my 15+ years of analytics experience has been applied across regulated financial services, government and enterprise environments. The Operational Risk Insights and Analytics Manager role at Macquarie Group is a natural convergence of that experience.

At Pepper Financial Services Group, I delivered executive dashboards tracking lending portfolio performance, operational efficiency and compliance KPIs — equipping senior leadership and risk teams with timely, data-driven insights to identify emerging risk trends in a highly regulated environment. I administered Tableau Server for 500+ users, maintained data-source governance under strict financial-services compliance standards, and designed SQL-based transformation layers supporting risk and operational reporting across the business.

At Foxtel, I pioneered the organisation's Gen AI adoption initiative — building the business case, securing stakeholder endorsement and delivering a pilot program using Claude-assisted analysis of customer segmentation data to accelerate insight turnaround and sharpen decision-making speed. This is not theoretical familiarity: I have built working applications and reporting dashboards using Claude Code, Streamlit, GitHub, Claude AI and King AI, and I am genuinely willing to demonstrate these live. My toolkit also includes Tableau, Power BI, Alteryx and Snowflake — all explicitly relevant to this role.

What differentiates my application is the combination of deep technical delivery and the ability to translate that work into clear narratives for executives and governance stakeholders — exactly the balance Macquarie's Operational Risk Strategy and Insights team requires. I understand that effective risk analytics is not just about accurate data; it is about communicating emerging risk in a way that drives informed, forward-looking decisions across personal banking, business banking and wealth management.

I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my background can add immediate value to your team. I am available at your convenience and happy to provide a live demonstration of my AI-assisted analytics work.

Kind regards,

Madeli van Niekerk
0424 998 470

cover_letter:
Dear Leon,

When a scaling technology business needs someone to walk into a room with senior leaders and challenge assumptions using data — not just report on it — the gap between a strong BI practitioner and a genuine strategic analytics leader becomes critical. That distinction is what drew me to this role.

Across 20 years working at the intersection of data and executive decision-making, I have consistently operated as more than a delivery function. At Foxtel, I owned the AI adoption strategy end-to-end — authoring the executive business case, securing C-suite sponsorship, and leading a Gen AI pilot that leveraged customer segmentation to accelerate strategic insight turnaround across multiple business units. That work required me to translate emerging capability into commercial opportunity and present a credible case for change to senior leadership. It is the kind of mandate this role describes.

At MIP, I served as lead analytics consultant to C-suite and senior government stakeholders — including NSW Health and Premier Cabinet — scoping engagements, owning client relationships, and translating complex data environments into strategic insight frameworks that informed executive decisions in regulated environments. That experience sharpened my ability to hold the analytical conversation at leadership level, not just support it from behind the scenes.

What I bring to this role specifically is the ability to work with large, complex datasets and turn them into narratives that land — not dashboards that sit on a server, but structured arguments that shape strategy. My emerging AI and Gen AI practitioner capability, combined with a track record of building the business case for analytics investment at executive level, positions me to contribute from day one in a newly created, C-suite-reporting mandate.

I would welcome the opportunity to speak with you directly about how my background maps to what this business needs as it scales. I am available at your convenience and can be reached on 0424 998 470.

Kind regards,

Madeli van Niekerk
0424 998 470

cover_letter:
Dear APJ Hiring Team,

Fifteen years of cross-sector consulting, executive advisory and structured business transformation — including hands-on evaluation of Gen AI outputs for strategic accuracy and relevance — is what I bring to this Management Consultant contract role.

At Foxtel, I led the organisation's AI adoption initiative end-to-end: building the business case, securing executive stakeholder buy-in, and critically, overseeing structured evaluation of Gen AI outputs for accuracy, alignment with business objectives and decision-making relevance before executive presentation. That experience — assessing AI-generated analysis against real-world consulting standards and identifying where outputs needed refinement — maps directly to what this role requires.

Across my consulting career at MIP, Moyo Business Advisory and Rethink Management Consulting, I delivered 10+ end-to-end engagements for C-suite clients including Sydney Water and NSW Health — applying structured analytical frameworks to define business problems, design reporting strategies and produce client-ready recommendations that drove operational and strategic outcomes. I also facilitated business process improvement workshops across government and enterprise organisations, identifying gaps, mapping current-state processes and delivering prioritised transformation recommendations aligned to strategic objectives. These are precisely the capabilities APJ needs to evaluate AI system outputs against real-world management consulting standards.

My Industrial Engineering background gives me rigorous systems-thinking foundations, and my 15 years operating at the intersection of strategy, data and executive decision-making means I can assess complex business scenarios, case studies and consulting frameworks with both analytical precision and practical credibility.

I am available for the flexible 10–40 hour weekly commitment this contract requires and am well-practised in delivering high-quality written and verbal feedback in remote, cross-functional environments.

I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my consulting experience and AI evaluation background can add immediate value to APJ's work. Please feel free to reach out at your convenience.

Kind regards,

Madeli van Niekerk
0424 998 470

**Recommendation 1 — Professional Summary Opening**

**Before:**
> Senior Data & Analytics Leader with 15+ years of experience delivering end-to-end data solutions across ETL/ELT pipelines, cloud data platforms and enterprise business intelligence.

**After:**
> Senior Insights Leader with 15+ years of experience translating complex subscriber, customer and audience data into clear, actionable insight that drives growth, retention and strategic decision-making across media, financial services and government.

**Why:** The job ad explicitly says the role is "not a deeply technical specialist role" — leading with ETL pipelines and cloud platforms signals the wrong profile and will cost the candidate interview consideration regardless of ATS score.

**Recommendation 2 — Summary Technical Tagline**

**Before:**
> BI Specialist · ETL & Data Engineering · Gen AI Practitioner

**After:**
> Insights Leadership · Subscriber & Audience Analytics · Team Development & Capability Building

**Why:** These three descriptors mirror the role's three core priorities and replace a tagline that actively misaligns the candidate with the job description.
